member.summary: Get or sets the value of .

member.name: P:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter.AzureMonitorExporterOptions.SamplingRatio
member.summary: Gets or sets the ratio of telemetry items to be sampled. The value must be between 0.0F and 1.0F, inclusive. For example, specifying 0.4 means that 40% of traces are sampled and 60% are dropped.

member.name: P:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter.AzureMonitorExporterOptions.TracesPerSecond
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of traces per second to be sampled when using rate-limited sampling. For example, specifying 0.5 means one request every two seconds. When both TracesPerSecond and SamplingRatio are specified, TracesPerSecond takes precedence.

member.name: P:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter.AzureMonitorExporterOptions.Version

member.summary: The versions of Azure Monitor supported by this client library.

member.name: P:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter.AzureMonitorExporterOptions.StorageDirectory
member.summary: Override the default directory for offline storage.

member.name: P:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter.AzureMonitorExporterOptions.DisableOfflineStorage
member.summary: Disable offline storage.

members.member:
Enables or disables the Live Metrics feature. This property is enabled by default. Note: Enabling Live Metrics incurs no additional billing or costs. However, it does introduce a performance overhead due to extra data collection, processing, and networking calls. This overhead is only significant when the LiveMetrics portal is actively used in the UI. Once the portal is closed,
LiveMetrics reverts to a 'silent' mode with minimal to no overhead.
This setting is applicable only when `UseAzureMonitorExporter` API is used. .

member.name: P:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter.AzureMonitorExporterOptions.EnableTraceBasedLogsSampler
member.summary: Enables or disables filtering logs based on trace sampling decisions.
member.remarks: When enabled, only logs associated with sampled traces are exported. Logs without trace context are always exported. This reduces log volume while maintaining trace-log correlation.

member.name: P:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter.AzureMonitorExporterOptions.EnableStatsbeat
member.summary: Internal flag to control if Statsbeat is enabled.

member.name: P:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter.AzureMonitorExporterOptions.EnableStandardMetrics
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether standard metrics should be collected. Default is true.

member.name: P:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter.AzureMonitorExporterOptions.EnablePerformanceCounters
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether performance counters should be collected. Default is true.

member.name: M:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter.Models.TelemetryItem.#ctor(System.String,System.DateTimeOffset)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of .
member.param: Type name of telemetry data item.

Event date time when telemetry item was created. This is the wall clock time on the client when the event was generated. There is no guarantee that the client's time is accurate. This field must be formatted in UTC ISO 8601 format, with a trailing 'Z' character, as described publicly on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#UTC. Note: the number of decimal seconds digits provided are variable (and unspecified). Consumers should handle this, i.e. managed code consumers should not use format 'O' for parsing as it specifies a fixed length. Example: 2009-06-15T13:45:30.0000000Z.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of .
member.param: Envelope version. For internal use only. By assigning this the default, it will not be serialized within the payload unless changed to a value other than #1.

member.name: T:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter.ExporterRegistrationHostedService
member.summary: A hosted service responsible for registering the Azure Monitor Trace and Log Exporters. It also initializes Live Metrics if enabled. Note: The Metric Exporter is not registered here — it is directly handled. via the API. Unlike traces and logs, the order of metric instrumentation and exporters does not impact registration. Metrics should be registered using the API.

member.name: M:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter.Internals.ApplicationInsightsSampler.ShouldSample(OpenTelemetry.Trace.SamplingParameters@)
member.summary: Computational method using the DJB2 Hash algorithm to decide whether to sample a given telemetry item, based on its Trace Id.

member.returns: Returns whether or not we should sample telemetry in the form of a class.

member.name: F:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter.Internals.ConnectionString.AadHelper.DefaultAadScope
member.summary: Default AAD Scope for Ingestion. IMPORTANT: This value only works in the Public Azure Cloud. For Sovereign Azure Clouds, this value MUST be built from the Connection String.

member.name: M:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter.Internals.ConnectionString.AadHelper.GetScope(System.String)
member.summary: Get the Scope value required for AAD authentication.
member.remarks: The AUDIENCE is a url that identifies Azure Monitor in a specific cloud (For example: "https://monitor.azure.com/"). The SCOPE is the audience + the permission (For example: "https://monitor.azure.com//.default").

member.name: M:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter.Internals.ConnectionString.ConnectionStringParser.GetValues(System.String)
member.summary: Parse a connection string that matches the format: "key1=value1;key2=value2;key3=value3". This method will encapsulate all exception handling.
member.remarks: Official Doc: .
member.exception: Any exceptions that occur while parsing the connection string will be wrapped and re-thrown.

member.name: M:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter.Internals.ConnectionString.ConnectionStringParser.GetEndpoint(Azure.Core.ConnectionString,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Evaluate connection string and return the requested endpoint.
member.remarks: Parsing the connection string MUST follow these rules: 1. check for explicit endpoint (location is ignored) 2. check for endpoint suffix (location is optional) 3. use default endpoint (location is ignored) This behavior is required by the Connection String Specification.

member.name: M:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter.Internals.ConnectionString.ConnectionStringParser.TryBuildUri(System.String,System.String,System.Uri@,System.String)
member.summary: Construct a Uri from the possible parts. Format: "location.prefix.suffix". Example: "https://westus2.dc.applicationinsights.azure.cn/".
member.remarks: Will also attempt to sanitize user input. Won't fail if the user typo-ed an extra period.

member.name: F:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter.Internals.ConnectionString.Constants.ConnectionStringMaxLength
member.summary: Maximum allowed length for connection string.
member.remarks: Currently 9 accepted keywords (~200 characters). Assuming 200 characters per value (~1800 characters). Total theoretical max length = (1800 + 200) = 2000. Setting an over-exaggerated max length to protect against malicious injections (2^12 = 4096).

member.name: T:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter.Internals.Diagnostics.AzureMonitorExporterEventSource
member.summary: EventSource for the AzureMonitorExporter. EventSource Guid at Runtime: bb5be13f-ec3a-5ab2-6a6a-0f881d6e0d5b. (This guid can be found by debugging this class and inspecting the "Log" singleton and reading the "Guid" property).
member.remarks:
PerfView Instructions:
To collect all events: PerfView.exe collect -MaxCollectSec:300 -NoGui /onlyProviders=*OpenTelemetry-AzureMonitor-Exporter
To collect events based on LogLevel: PerfView.exe collect -MaxCollectSec:300 -NoGui /onlyProviders:OpenTelemetry-AzureMonitor-Exporter::Verbose
Dotnet-Trace Instructions:
To collect all events: dotnet-trace collect --process-id PID --providers OpenTelemetry-AzureMonitor-Exporter
To collect events based on LogLevel: dotnet-trace collect --process-id PID --providers OpenTelemetry-AzureMonitor-Exporter::Verbose
Logman Instructions:
Create a text file containing providers: echo "{bb5be13f-ec3a-5ab2-6a6a-0f881d6e0d5b}" > providers.txt
Start collecting: logman -start exporter -pf providers.txt -ets -bs 1024 -nb 100 256
Stop collecting: logman -stop exporter -ets

member.name: T:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter.Internals.Diagnostics.TelemetryItemOrigin
member.summary: This is used for internal logs, to distinguish where a TelemetryItem originated from.
member.remarks: Because each Exporter can have a unique configuration, it's possible for one Exporter to have issues not experienced by any others in a process.

Optional blob provider used to save new blobs.
Existing blob (when retransmitting from storage).
Connection vars for logging.
Origin of telemetry (Exporter vs Storage).
If AAD auth is enabled.
Telemetry counter for tracking metrics.
member.returns: TransmissionResult describing actions taken.

member.name: T:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter.Internals.SchemaConstants
member.summary:
This class encapsulates constant values from the Azure Monitor Swagger
and the Ingestion service data models . NOTE: Some of these max lengths are not defined in the Swagger. Must refer to the internal service repo.
member.remarks: MaxLength (inclusive) defines the maximum number of characters. LessThanDays (exclusive) defines the upper limit of days. MaxFrames (inclusive) defines the maximum number of frames.

member.name: F:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter.Internals.SchemaConstants.KVP_MaxKeyLength
member.remarks: If a Key exceeds maximum allowable length limit, then that particular Key-Value pair should be dropped.

member.name: F:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter.Internals.SchemaConstants.Duration_MaxValue
member.remarks: Represents 1 tick (1 ten-millionth of a second) less than 1000 days.

member.name: F:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter.Internals.SchemaConstants.GenAi_Properties_MaxValueLength
member.summary: Maximum value length for GenAI semantic convention properties (256 KB). These properties may carry large payloads (e.g. full prompt/completion content) and are truncated to a higher limit than standard properties.

member.name: F:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter.Internals.SchemaConstants.GenAiProperties
member.summary: GenAI semantic convention property keys that receive a higher truncation limit.

member.name: M:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter.Internals.StackFrameExtensions.GetMethodWithoutWarning(System.Diagnostics.StackFrame)
member.summary: Wrapper for . This method disables the Trimmer warning "IL2026:RequiresUnreferencedCode". Callers MUST handle the null condition.
member.remarks: In an AOT scenario GetMethod() will return null. Note this can happen even in non AOT scenarios. Instead, call ToString() which gives a string like this: "MethodName + 0x00 at offset 000 in file:line:column filename unknown:0:0".

member.name: P:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.LiveMetrics.AzureMonitorLiveMetricsOptions.ConnectionString
member.summary: The Connection String provides users with a single configuration setting to identify the Azure Monitor resource and endpoint.

member.name: P:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.LiveMetrics.AzureMonitorLiveMetricsOptions.Credential
member.summary: Get or sets the value of . If is not set, AAD authentication is disabled and Instrumentation Key from the Connection String will be used.

member.name: P:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.LiveMetrics.AzureMonitorLiveMetricsOptions.EnableLiveMetrics
member.summary: Enables or disables the Live Metrics feature. This property is enabled by default. Note: Enabling Live Metrics incurs no additional billing or costs. However, it does introduce a performance overhead due to extra data collection, processing, and networking calls. This overhead is only significant when the LiveMetrics portal is actively used in the UI. Once the portal is closed, LiveMetrics reverts to a 'silent' mode with minimal to no overhead. .
SDK ping.
member.param: The ikey of the target Application Insights component that displays server info sent by /QuickPulseService.svc/ping.
Deprecated. An alternative way to pass api key. Use AAD auth instead.
Timestamp when SDK transmits the metrics and documents to QuickPulse. A 8-byte long type of ticks.
Computer name where AI SDK lives. QuickPulse uses machine name with instance name as a backup.
Service instance name where AI SDK lives. QuickPulse uses machine name with instance name as a backup.
Identifies an AI SDK as trusted agent to report metrics and documents.
Cloud role name for which SDK reports metrics and documents.

member.name: T:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.LiveMetrics.Internals.DataCollection.DocumentHelper
member.summary: Helper class used to convert Activity to the LiveMetrics document schema.

member.name: T:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.LiveMetrics.Internals.DataCollection.DoubleBuffer
member.summary: Implements a double buffering mechanism for handling DocumentIngress objects. This allows for concurrent writes to one buffer while the other can be read from or processed. The 'WriteDocument' method is used to add documents to the current active buffer. The 'FlipDocumentBuffers' method swaps the current buffer with a new one, allowing the consumer to process the documents in the returned buffer without interference from ongoing writes.

member.name: T:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.LiveMetrics.Internals.DataCollection.LiveMetricsBuffer
member.summary: This struct encapsulates pre-aggregated metrics sent to Live Metrics.

member.name: T:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.LiveMetrics.Internals.Filtering.AccumulatedValues
member.summary: Accumulator for calculated metrics.

member.name: T:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.LiveMetrics.Internals.Filtering.CollectionConfiguration
member.summary: Represents the collection configuration - a set of calculated metrics, and full telemetry documents to be collected by the SDK.
member.remarks: This class is a hub for all pieces of configurable collection configuration. Upon initialization - it creates collection-time instances of and maintains them in separate collections by telemetry type. These are used to filter and calculated calculated metrics configured by the service. - it creates certain metadata collections which are used by other collection-time components to learn more about what is being collected at any given time.

member.name: P:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.LiveMetrics.Internals.Filtering.CollectionConfiguration.TelemetryMetadata
member.summary: Gets Telemetry types only. Used by QuickPulseTelemetryProcessor.

member.name: P:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.LiveMetrics.Internals.Filtering.CollectionConfiguration.DocumentStreams
member.summary: Gets document streams. Telemetry items are provided by QuickPulseTelemetryProcessor.

member.name: T:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.LiveMetrics.Internals.Filtering.DerivedMetric`1
member.summary: Represents a single configured metric that needs to be calculated and reported on top of the telemetry items that pass through the pipeline. Includes a set of filters that define which telemetry items to consider, a projection which defines which field to use as a value, and an aggregation which dictates the algorithm of arriving at a single reportable value within a second.

member.name: T:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.LiveMetrics.Internals.Filtering.DocumentStream
member.summary: Represents a concept of a uniquely identifiable set of full telemetry documents that are being reported by the SDK. The notion of a stream is needed since multiple UX sessions might be querying for full telemetry documents with different filtering criteria simultaneously.

member.name: T:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.LiveMetrics.Internals.Filtering.Filter`1
member.summary: Filter determines whether a telemetry document matches the criterion. The filter's configuration (condition) is specified in a DTO.

member.name: T:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.LiveMetrics.Internals.Filtering.FilterConjunctionGroup`1
member.summary: Defines an AND group of filters.

member.returns: true if there's still quota left, false otherwise.

member.name: M:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.LiveMetrics.Internals.GuidExtensions.ToStringInvariant(System.Guid,System.String)
member.summary: Overload for Guid.ToString().
member.remarks: This method encapsulates the language switch for NetStandard and NetFramework and resolves the error "The behavior of guid.ToString() could vary based on the current user's locale settings"

member.name: T:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.LiveMetrics.Internals.LiveMetricConstants.MetricId
member.summary: These are the values that are sent to Application Insights Live Metrics.

member.name: T:Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.LiveMetrics.Internals.LiveMetricsClientManager
member.summary: This partial class encapsulates all the metrics that are tracked and reported to the Live Metrics service.
This partial class encapsulates the Ping and Post methods. This is the data that is sent to the Live Metrics service.
This partial class encapsulates the State Machine. This controls if we are in either the Ping or Post state.
member.remarks: RULES FOR BACKOFF: POST: We expect to Post once every second, but no longer than once every 20 seconds. If we exceed the 20 seconds, we consider this a failure and Stop Posting. We will wait 60 seconds before Pinging again. PING: We expect to Ping once every 5 seconds, but no longer than once every 60 seconds. If we exceed the 60 seconds, we will wait 60 seconds before Pinging again.

remarks.para: For Memory: . "The amount of memory, in bytes, allocated for the associated process that cannot be shared with other processes.".
For CPU: . "A TimeSpan that indicates the amount of time that the associated process has spent utilizing the CPU. This value is the sum of the UserProcessorTime and the PrivilegedProcessorTime.".

member.summary: Notifies that amount of data was written to the output /.
member.exception: Thrown when attempting to advance past the end of the underlying buffer.
member.remarks: You must request a new buffer after calling Advance to continue writing more data and cannot write to a previously acquired buffer.

member.remarks: This will never return an empty .
There is no guarantee that successive calls will return the same buffer or the same-sized buffer.
You must request a new buffer after calling Advance to continue writing more data and cannot write to a previously acquired buffer.

member.name: M:Azure.Core.AsyncLockWithValue`1.GetLockOrValueAsync(System.Boolean,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Method that either returns cached value or acquire a lock. If one caller has acquired a lock, other callers will be waiting for the lock to be released. If value is set, lock is released and all waiters get that value. If value isn't set, the next waiter in the queue will get the lock.

member.name: P:Azure.Core.AsyncLockWithValue`1.LockOrValue.HasValue
member.summary: Returns true if lock contains the cached value. Otherwise false.

member.name: P:Azure.Core.AsyncLockWithValue`1.LockOrValue.Value
member.summary: Returns cached value if it was set when lock has been created. Throws exception otherwise.

member.name: M:Azure.Core.AsyncLockWithValue`1.LockOrValue.SetValue(`0)
member.summary: Set value to the cache and to all the waiters.

member.name: M:Azure.Core.Pipeline.ClientDiagnostics.#ctor(Azure.Core.ClientOptions,System.Nullable{System.Boolean})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The customer provided client options object.

Flag controlling if created by this for client method calls should be suppressed when called by other Azure SDK client methods. It's recommended to set it to true for new clients; use default (null) for backward compatibility reasons, or set it to false to explicitly disable suppression for specific cases. The default value could change in the future, the flag should be only set to false if suppression for the client should never be enabled.

member.name: M:Azure.Core.OperationInternal.#ctor(Azure.Core.IOperation,Azure.Core.Pipeline.ClientDiagnostics,Azure.Response,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Collections.Generic.KeyValuePair{System.String,System.String}},Azure.Core.DelayStrategy)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The long-running operation making use of this class. Passing "this" is expected.
Used for diagnostic scope and exception creation. This is expected to be the instance created during the construction of your main client.

The initial value of . Usually, long-running operation objects can be instantiated in two ways:
When calling a client's "Start<OperationName>" method, a service call is made to start the operation, and an instance is returned. In this case, the response received from this service call can be passed here.
When a user instantiates an directly using a public constructor, there's no previous service call. In this case, passing null is expected.
The type name of the long-running operation making use of this class. Used when creating diagnostic scopes. If left null, the type name will be inferred based on the parameter .
The attributes to use during diagnostic scope creation.
The delay strategy to use. Default is .

public Response GetRawResponse() => _operationInternal.RawResponse;

member.summary:
Calls the server to get the latest status of the long-running operation, handling diagnostic scope creation for distributed tracing. The default scope name can be changed with the "operationTypeName" parameter passed to the constructor. Usage example:
public async ValueTask<Response> UpdateStatusAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken) => await _operationInternal.UpdateStatusAsync(cancellationToken).ConfigureAwait(false);
member.param: A controlling the request lifetime.
member.returns: The HTTP response received from the server.
member.remarks: After a successful run, this method will update and might update .
member.exception: Thrown if there's been any issues during the connection, or if the operation has completed with failures.

member.name: M:Azure.Core.OperationInternalBase.WaitForCompletionResponseAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Periodically calls until the long-running operation completes. After each service call, a retry-after header may be returned to communicate that there is no reason to poll for status change until the specified time has passed. The maximum of the retry after value and the fallback strategy is then used as the wait interval. Headers supported are: "Retry-After", "retry-after-ms", and "x-ms-retry-after-ms", Usage example:
public async ValueTask<Response<T>> WaitForCompletionAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken) => await _operationInternal.WaitForCompletionAsync(cancellationToken).ConfigureAwait(false);
member.param: A controlling the request lifetime.
member.returns: The last HTTP response received from the server, including the final result of the long-running operation.
member.exception: Thrown if there's been any issues during the connection, or if the operation has completed with failures.

member.name: M:Azure.Core.OperationInternalBase.WaitForCompletionResponseAsync(System.TimeSpan,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Periodically calls until the long-running operation completes. The interval between calls is defined by the parameter , but it can change based on information returned from the server. After each service call, a retry-after header may be returned to communicate that there is no reason to poll for status change until the specified time has passed. In this case, the maximum value between the parameter and the retry-after header is chosen as the wait interval. Headers supported are: "Retry-After", "retry-after-ms", and "x-ms-retry-after-ms". Usage example:
public async ValueTask<Response<T>> WaitForCompletionAsync(TimeSpan pollingInterval, CancellationToken cancellationToken) => await _operationInternal.WaitForCompletionAsync(pollingInterval, cancellationToken).ConfigureAwait(false);
member.param: The interval between status requests to the server.
A controlling the request lifetime.
member.returns: The last HTTP response received from the server, including the final result of the long-running operation.
member.exception: Thrown if there's been any issues during the connection, or if the operation has completed with failures.

member.summary: Indicates that an API is experimental and it may change in the future.
member.remarks: This attribute allows call sites to be flagged with a diagnostic that indicates that an experimental feature is used. Authors can use this attribute to ship preview features in their assemblies.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class, specifying the ID that the compiler will use when reporting a use of the API the attribute applies to.
member.param: The ID that the compiler will use when reporting a use of the API the attribute applies to.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute.DiagnosticId
member.summary: Gets the ID that the compiler will use when reporting a use of the API the attribute applies to.
member.remarks:
The diagnostic ID is shown in build output for warnings and errors.
This property represents the unique ID that can be used to suppress the warnings or errors, if needed.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute.UrlFormat
member.summary: Gets or sets the URL for corresponding documentation. The API accepts a format string instead of an actual URL, creating a generic URL that includes the diagnostic ID.
member.value: The format string that represents a URL to corresponding documentation.
member.remarks: An example format string is https://contoso.com/obsoletion-warnings/{0}.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the specified method requires the ability to generate new code at runtime, for example through .
member.remarks: This allows tools to understand which methods are unsafe to call when compiling ahead of time.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified message.
member.param: A message that contains information about the usage of dynamic code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute.Message
member.summary: Gets a message that contains information about the usage of dynamic code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute.Url
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional URL that contains more information about the method, why it requires dynamic code, and what options a consumer has to deal with it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the specified method requires dynamic access to code that is not referenced statically, for example through .
member.remarks: This allows tools to understand which methods are unsafe to call when removing unreferenced code from an application.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified message.
member.param: A message that contains information about the usage of unreferenced code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute.Message
member.summary: Gets a message that contains information about the usage of unreferenced code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute.Url
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional URL that contains more information about the method, why it requires unreferenced code, and what options a consumer has to deal with it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute
member.summary: Suppresses reporting of a specific rule violation, allowing multiple suppressions on a single code artifact.
member.remarks: is different than in that it doesn't have a . So it is always preserved in the compiled assembly.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class, specifying the category of the tool and the identifier for an analysis rule.
member.param: The category for the attribute.
The identifier of the analysis rule the attribute applies to.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Category
member.summary: Gets the category identifying the classification of the attribute.
member.remarks: The property describes the tool or tool analysis category for which a message suppression attribute applies.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.CheckId
member.summary: Gets the identifier of the analysis tool rule to be suppressed.
member.remarks: Concatenated together, the and properties form a unique check identifier.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Scope
member.summary: Gets or sets the scope of the code that is relevant for the attribute.
member.remarks: The Scope property is an optional argument that specifies the metadata scope for which the attribute is relevant.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Target
member.summary: Gets or sets a fully qualified path that represents the target of the attribute.
member.remarks: The property is an optional argument identifying the analysis target of the attribute. An example value is "System.IO.Stream.ctor():System.Void". Because it is fully qualified, it can be long, particularly for targets such as parameters. The analysis tool user interface should be capable of automatically formatting the parameter.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.MessageId
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional argument expanding on exclusion criteria.
member.remarks: The property is an optional argument that specifies additional exclusion where the literal metadata target is not sufficiently precise. For example, the cannot be applied within a method, and it may be desirable to suppress a violation against a statement in the method that will give a rule violation, but not against all statements in the method.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Justification
member.summary: Gets or sets the justification for suppressing the code analysis message.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicDependencyAttribute
member.summary: States a dependency that one member has on another.
member.remarks: This can be used to inform tooling of a dependency that is otherwise not evident purely from metadata and IL, for example a member relied on via reflection.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMembersAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that certain members on a specified are accessed dynamically, for example through .
member.remarks:
This allows tools to understand which members are being accessed during the execution of a program.
This attribute is valid on members whose type is or .
When this attribute is applied to a location of type , the assumption is that the string represents a fully qualified type name.
When this attribute is applied to a class, interface, or struct, the members specified can be accessed dynamically on instances returned from calling on instances of that class, interface, or struct.
If the attribute is applied to a method it's treated as a special case and it implies the attribute should be applied to the "this" parameter of the method. As such the attribute should only be used on instance methods of types assignable to System.Type (or string, but no methods will use it there).
